text: The robotic fourth arm grasps the cornea, incorporating the round ligament at a perpendicular angle, and applies cranial superior traction to expose the triangle of freedom. The ovarian vessels are grasped with medial traction, allowing entry into the retroperitoneum.

The surgeon extends the incision using a spread and lift technique parallel to the ovarian vessels. Areolar tissue is identified and meticulously dissected inferiorly until the posterior peritoneum is visible.

The surgeon uses a grasper to apply counter traction while employing a push and spread technique to carefully advance towards the ureter, ensuring precise dissection along the identified anatomical path.
The surgeon identifies the ureter and gently probes and spreads the superior areolar tissue to clear the path, ensuring no trauma to the uterine vessels.
